facts.text: Kingston, New Jersey, first appeared in 1683 as a ford over the Millstone River, making it one of the state’s oldest communities.
The Delaware and Raritan Canal, completed in 1834, turned Kingston into a key transportation hub for farmers and factories.
In 1839, the Camden and Amboy Railroad extended its line to Kingston, linking the village to New Brunswick and boosting commerce.
The Kingston Library Company, founded in 1812, was the earliest public library in the area, offering 284 books to 70 members for just $1 a year.
Kingston sits along the Millstone River, which forms the Mercer County line to the west and has long supported local mills and taverns.
The Kingston Village Advisory Committee, created by the New Jersey State Planning Commission in 2001, is the only village center officially designated by the state.
